_comment: Exercise catalog for the home/desk fitness app. MET values sourced from the Compendium of Physical Activities (Ainsworth et al. 2011) — https://pacompendium.com/. When an exact match is unavailable, the nearest analog is used and noted in the exercise's 'notes' field. All exercises are equipment-free and assume only chair, wall, stairs, or doorway are available.

calorie_model.notes: Apply session.intensity multiplier. Rest seconds NOT counted as work time. For rep-based exercises: work_seconds = sets * amount * seconds_per_rep.

exercises.name: Neck Rolls
instructions: Sit or stand tall with shoulders relaxed.
Drop chin toward chest.
Slowly roll head to the right shoulder, then back, then left, then forward in a smooth circle.
Reverse direction after each full rotation.
common_mistakes: Forcing the head too far back

modifications.easier: Half-circles only (chin to shoulder, no backward arc)
modifications.harder: Hold each position for 3 seconds

exercises.name: Shoulder Rolls
instructions: Sit or stand with arms relaxed at your sides.
Roll both shoulders up, back, down, and forward in a smooth circle.
After 10 reps, reverse direction.
common_mistakes: Small, jerky motion instead of full circles

modifications.harder: Add light arm extension during the back phase

exercises.name: Chin Tucks
instructions: Sit upright. Look straight ahead.
Without tilting your head, draw your chin straight back, creating a 'double chin'.
Hold 2 seconds, release.
Repeat.
common_mistakes: Tilting head down instead of pulling straight back

modifications.harder: Hold 5 seconds, add gentle finger resistance against chin

exercises.notes: Critical posture exercise for forward-head posture from screen work.

exercises.name: Seated Spinal Twist
instructions: Sit tall in a chair, feet flat on the floor.
Place your right hand on the outside of your left knee.
Inhale to lengthen the spine; exhale and rotate your torso to the left.
Hold, breathing deeply.
Switch sides.
common_mistakes: Yanking with the arm instead of rotating from the spine

modifications.harder: Hold 30+ seconds, add a deeper twist on each exhale

exercises.name: Seated Cat-Cow
instructions: Sit tall, hands on knees.
Inhale: arch back, push chest forward, look slightly up (cow).
Exhale: round back, tuck chin to chest, pull navel in (cat).
Move slowly with the breath.
common_mistakes: Moving only the head instead of the whole spine

modifications.harder: Add arm reach overhead during cow phase

exercises.name: Wrist Circles
instructions: Extend arms forward.
Make slow, full circles with the wrists.
Reverse direction after 10 reps.
Optionally interlace fingers and rotate.

modifications.harder: Add wrist flexion/extension stretches between sets

exercises.notes: Critical for keyboard/mouse users.

exercises.name: 20-20-20 Eye Break
instructions: Look away from your screen.
Focus on an object at least 20 feet (6 meters) away for 20 seconds.
Blink slowly several times.

modifications.harder: Close eyes and gently massage temples afterward

exercises.notes: Recommended every 20 minutes of screen time by the American Optometric Association.

exercises.name: Standing Back Extension
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart.
Place hands on your lower back, fingers pointing down.
Gently arch backward, lifting the chest.
Hold 2 seconds, return to neutral.

modifications.harder: Hold 5 seconds at the top

exercises.notes: Counters the forward-flexed posture of sitting.

exercises.name: Seated Forward Fold
instructions: Sit on the edge of your chair, feet flat on the floor.
Hinge from the hips and fold your torso over your thighs.
Let arms dangle toward the floor.
Breathe deeply, releasing tension with each exhale.

modifications.harder: Extend legs straight and reach toward toes

exercises.name: Ankle Circles
instructions: Sit and lift one foot off the floor.
Rotate the ankle in slow, full circles.
Reverse direction after 10 reps.
Switch feet.
common_mistakes: Moving the whole leg instead of just the ankle

modifications.harder: Spell the alphabet with your foot

exercises.notes: Improves circulation in the lower legs after long sitting.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Extensions
instructions: Sit upright in a chair, feet flat.
Slowly extend one leg until straight, hold 1 second.
Lower with control.
Alternate legs.

modifications.harder: Hold extended position 3 seconds, both legs at once

exercises.name: Doorway Chest Stretch
instructions: Stand in a doorway.
Place forearms on the door frame, elbows at shoulder height.
Step one foot through the doorway, leaning forward gently.
Feel a stretch across the chest. Hold.

modifications.harder: Raise elbows higher to target the lower chest

exercises.notes: Powerful counter to rounded-shoulder posture from typing.

exercises.name: Standing Hip Flexor Lunge Stretch
instructions: Step into a long lunge position, back knee soft.
Tuck pelvis under (posterior tilt) to feel the stretch in the front of the back hip.
Hold, breathing deeply.
Switch sides.

modifications.harder: Lower back knee toward the floor; raise opposite arm overhead

exercises.notes: Hip flexors shorten dramatically with prolonged sitting.

exercises.name: Side Neck Stretch
instructions: Sit or stand tall.
Drop your right ear toward your right shoulder.
Gently rest your right hand on the left side of your head (no pulling).
Hold, then switch sides.
common_mistakes: Lifting the shoulder toward the ear

modifications.harder: Reach the opposite arm down and away to deepen the stretch

exercises.name: Scapular Squeezes
instructions: Sit or stand tall with arms at your sides.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together as if pinching a pencil between them.
Hold 2 seconds, release.
Repeat.

modifications.harder: Arms in 'goalpost' position (elbows at shoulder height, bent 90 degrees)

exercises.notes: Activates the rhomboids and middle trapezius — under-used in desk workers.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squats
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out.
Hinge at the hips and bend the knees, lowering as if sitting in a chair.
Keep chest up and weight in heels.
Descend until thighs are parallel to floor (or as low as comfortable).
Drive through heels to stand.

modifications.easier: Box squats (sit briefly on a chair each rep)
modifications.harder: Tempo squats — 3 seconds down, 1 second pause, 1 second up

exercises.name: Sumo Squats
instructions: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width, toes pointed out 45 degrees.
Lower into a squat keeping the torso upright.
Press through the heels to return to standing.

modifications.harder: Pulse 3 times at the bottom of each rep

exercises.name: Reverse Lunges
instructions: Stand tall, feet hip-width.
Step one foot back into a long stance.
Lower the back knee toward the floor; front thigh parallel to floor.
Push through the front heel to return.
Alternate legs (10 each = 20 total).
common_mistakes: Front knee tracking past the toes

modifications.harder: Add a knee drive at the top of each rep

exercises.notes: Easier on the knees than forward lunges; recommended starting variant.

exercises.name: Side Lunges
instructions: Stand with feet together.
Step one foot wide to the side, bending that knee while keeping the other leg straight.
Push the hips back as you lower.
Drive through the bent leg's heel to return to start.
Alternate sides.

modifications.harder: Hold the bottom for 2 seconds

exercises.name: Single-Leg Deadlifts
instructions: Stand on one leg, slight bend in the knee.
Hinge from the hip, extending the free leg straight back.
Lower torso until parallel to the floor (or as far as control allows).
Squeeze the standing-leg glute to return.
Switch legs after the set.
common_mistakes: Bending the standing knee too much

modifications.easier: Touch the floor with fingertips for balance, or hold a wall
modifications.harder: Slow tempo (3 sec down, 3 sec up)

exercises.name: Glute Bridges
instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat on the floor close to glutes.
Press through heels and lift hips until shoulders, hips, and knees form a straight line.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top.
Lower with control.
common_mistakes: Arching the lower back instead of squeezing glutes

modifications.harder: Hold the top for 3 seconds; pulse

exercises.notes: Reactivates glutes that go dormant from sitting.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Glute Bridges
instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat.
Extend one leg straight out.
Press through the heel of the planted foot to lift hips.
Keep hips level — don't let the raised side drop.
Switch legs after the set.

modifications.easier: Free foot resting on the planted shin instead of extended
modifications.harder: Pause 2 seconds at the top

exercises.name: Calf Raises
instructions: Stand tall, feet hip-width apart.
Press up onto the balls of the feet, lifting the heels as high as possible.
Pause briefly at the top.
Lower with control.

modifications.harder: Single-leg version, or stand on a stair edge for greater range

exercises.name: Wall Sits
instructions: Stand with your back against a wall, feet about 2 feet out.
Slide down until thighs are parallel to the floor (90-degree knees).
Press lower back into the wall.
Hold for the prescribed time.

modifications.harder: Single-leg wall sit, or extend arms forward

exercises.name: Step-Ups
instructions: Stand in front of a sturdy chair or low step.
Place one foot fully on the surface.
Drive through that heel to lift your body up.
Step down with control.
Alternate legs (10 each = 20 total).
common_mistakes: Pushing off with the trailing leg instead of driving through the lead heel

modifications.harder: Add a knee drive at the top of each rep

exercises.notes: Use a stable chair against a wall, or a stair step.

exercises.name: Standard Push-Ups
instructions: Start in a high plank: hands under shoulders, body in a straight line.
Brace your core and squeeze glutes.
Lower chest toward the floor, elbows at ~45 degrees from torso.
Press back up to start.

modifications.harder: Slow tempo, decline push-ups

exercises.notes: MET varies; 3.8 is moderate effort, vigorous effort is closer to 8.0.

exercises.name: Knee Push-Ups
instructions: Start on all fours, hands under shoulders.
Walk hands forward so the body forms a straight line from knees to head.
Lower chest to the floor with elbows ~45 degrees.
Press back up.

modifications.harder: Standard push-ups

exercises.name: Incline Push-Ups
instructions: Place hands on a stable chair seat or sturdy elevated surface.
Walk feet back into a plank position.
Lower chest toward the surface.
Press back up.

modifications.harder: Lower surface; eventually move to floor

exercises.notes: Best progression for those who can't do standard push-ups yet.

exercises.name: Decline Push-Ups
instructions: Place feet on a sturdy chair.
Hands on the floor in plank position.
Lower chest to the floor.
Press back up while keeping body straight.

modifications.harder: Higher elevation; pause at the bottom

exercises.name: Wide-Grip Push-Ups
instructions: Start in plank with hands wider than shoulders.
Lower chest toward the floor.
Press back up.
common_mistakes: Going too wide and stressing the shoulders

modifications.harder: Pause 2 seconds at the bottom

exercises.name: Diamond Push-Ups
instructions: In plank position, place hands together so thumbs and index fingers form a diamond.
Lower chest toward the diamond, elbows tracking close to the body.
Press back up.

modifications.harder: Pause at the bottom

exercises.name: Wall Push-Ups
instructions: Stand facing a wall, arms extended, hands flat on the wall at shoulder height.
Step back until your weight leans into your hands.
Bend elbows to bring your chest toward the wall.
Press back to start.

modifications.harder: Step further back; eventually move to incline push-ups

exercises.notes: Easiest push-up progression — appropriate for absolute beginners.

exercises.name: Pike Push-Ups
instructions: Start in a downward-dog position: hips piked high, hands and feet on the floor.
Bend elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor.
Press back up to the starting position.
common_mistakes: Bending knees instead of hinging at hips

modifications.harder: Feet elevated on a chair

exercises.notes: Bodyweight progression toward overhead pressing strength.

exercises.name: Chair Tricep Dips
instructions: Sit on the edge of a sturdy chair, hands gripping the front edge next to your hips.
Slide off the chair, supporting weight with your arms.
Bend elbows to lower your hips toward the floor.
Press back up by extending the elbows.
common_mistakes: Shoulders shrugging up to the ears

modifications.harder: Legs straight out, heels on the floor

exercises.notes: Use a chair that won't tip — back against a wall is ideal.

exercises.name: Inchworms
instructions: Stand tall, feet hip-width.
Hinge forward and place hands on the floor (bend knees if needed).
Walk hands forward into a plank position.
Optional: do one push-up.
Walk hands back to feet and stand up.

modifications.easier: Bend knees more, skip the push-up
modifications.harder: Add a push-up at the bottom

exercises.notes: Excellent dynamic warmup; full-body.

exercises.name: Forearm Plank
instructions: Start on forearms with elbows under shoulders.
Extend legs back, balls of the feet on the floor.
Form a straight line from heels to head.
Squeeze glutes, brace core, breathe.

modifications.harder: Lift one foot off the floor at a time

exercises.name: Side Plank
instructions: Lie on your side with your forearm on the floor, elbow under shoulder.
Stack feet, lift hips so the body forms a straight line.
Hold; switch sides.

modifications.easier: Knees bent, supporting on bottom knee instead of feet
modifications.harder: Lift the top leg

exercises.name: Flutter Kicks
instructions: Lie on your back, hands tucked under your hips for lower back support.
Lift legs a few inches off the floor.
Press lower back into the floor.
Make small, quick alternating kicks (one leg up, the other slightly down).
common_mistakes: Lower back arching off the floor

modifications.harder: Slow tempo, larger range

exercises.name: Lying Leg Raises
instructions: Lie on your back, legs straight, hands at your sides or under your hips.
Press lower back into the floor.
Slowly lift legs to vertical (or as high as comfortable).
Lower with control without touching the floor between reps.

modifications.harder: Stop legs 2 inches above floor between reps

exercises.name: Bicycle Crunches
instructions: Lie on your back, hands lightly behind your head, knees lifted to tabletop.
Bring right elbow toward left knee while extending right leg.
Switch sides in a smooth pedaling motion.
Each side counts as one rep.

modifications.harder: Pause and squeeze at each rotation

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
instructions: Start in a high plank position.
Drive one knee toward your chest.
Quickly switch legs in a running motion while keeping hips low and steady.

modifications.easier: Slow tempo, hands on a chair (incline)
modifications.harder: Cross-body climbers (knee toward opposite elbow)

exercises.notes: Cardio + core hybrid; can be moderate or vigorous depending on pace.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
instructions: Lie on your back, arms extended toward the ceiling, knees bent at 90 degrees over hips.
Press lower back into the floor.
Slowly extend the right leg and lower the left arm overhead.
Return to start; switch sides.
common_mistakes: Lower back arching off the floor

modifications.easier: Move only the legs OR only the arms
modifications.harder: Pause 2 seconds at full extension

exercises.notes: Excellent for spine-safe core work; recommended for anyone with lower back issues.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
instructions: Start on all fours, hands under shoulders, knees under hips.
Brace your core.
Simultaneously extend the right arm forward and left leg back until they're parallel to the floor.
Hold 1 second, return.
Switch sides.

modifications.easier: Move only one limb at a time
modifications.harder: Hold each rep 3-5 seconds

exercises.name: Hollow Hold
instructions: Lie on your back.
Press lower back firmly into the floor.
Lift legs and shoulders off the floor, arms extended overhead alongside the ears.
Hold the 'banana' shape, breathing shallowly.
common_mistakes: Lower back lifting off the floor

modifications.easier: Knees tucked, arms at sides (tuck hold)
modifications.harder: Add small rocking motion (hollow rocks)

exercises.name: V-Ups
instructions: Lie flat on your back, arms extended overhead, legs straight.
Simultaneously lift legs and torso, reaching hands toward feet to form a V.
Lower with control.

modifications.easier: Tuck-ups (bend knees in instead of straight legs)
modifications.harder: Pause at the top

exercises.name: Scissor Kicks
instructions: Lie on your back, legs straight, hands under hips.
Lift both legs a few inches off the floor.
Cross one leg over the other, then switch in a scissoring motion.
Keep lower back pressed to the floor.

modifications.harder: Larger range or slower tempo

exercises.name: Plank Shoulder Taps
instructions: Start in a high plank position, feet slightly wider than hip-width for stability.
Slowly tap right hand to left shoulder.
Return, then tap left hand to right shoulder.
Keep hips as still as possible — minimize rocking.

modifications.harder: Feet closer together

exercises.name: Jumping Jacks
instructions: Stand with feet together, arms at sides.
Jump feet wide while raising arms overhead.
Jump back to start.
Land softly on the balls of the feet.

modifications.harder: Increase pace; add a clap overhead

exercises.notes: Loud — apartment dwellers should consider step-jacks.

exercises.name: High Knees
instructions: Stand tall.
Run in place, driving knees up to hip height.
Pump arms in opposition.
Stay light on the balls of the feet.

modifications.easier: March in place with high knees
modifications.harder: Increase pace, drive higher

exercises.name: Butt Kicks
instructions: Stand tall.
Run in place, kicking heels up toward your glutes.
Stay light on the balls of the feet.

modifications.harder: Maximum speed

exercises.name: Burpees
instructions: Stand tall.
Drop into a squat and place hands on the floor.
Jump or step feet back into a plank.
Optional push-up.
Jump or step feet forward to the hands.
Explode up into a jump with arms overhead.

modifications.easier: Step back instead of jumping; no push-up; no jump at the end
modifications.harder: Add a push-up; add a tuck jump

exercises.notes: High intensity — drops from list if user has any joint concerns.

exercises.name: Squat Jumps
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat.
Explode up into a jump, reaching arms up.
Land softly back into a squat.

modifications.easier: Bodyweight squats with a calf raise at the top
modifications.harder: Single-leg squat jumps

exercises.name: Lunge Jumps
instructions: Start in a lunge position.
Explode up, switching legs in mid-air.
Land softly in the opposite lunge.
Each side counts as one rep.

modifications.harder: Higher jumps, faster tempo

exercises.name: Star Jumps
instructions: Squat down, hands near feet.
Explode up, extending arms and legs into a star shape mid-air.
Land softly back into a squat.
common_mistakes: Not extending fully at the top

modifications.harder: Increase pace

exercises.name: Shadow Boxing
instructions: Stand in a boxing stance: feet shoulder-width, lead foot forward, hands up by face.
Throw a series of punches: jab, cross, hook.
Move your feet — bob, weave, pivot.
Keep continuous motion.

modifications.harder: Add a squat or burpee every 30 seconds

exercises.notes: Apartment-friendly cardio alternative to jumping.

exercises.name: Plank Jacks
instructions: Start in a high plank.
Jump feet wide, then back together (like a horizontal jumping jack).
Keep hips low and stable.

modifications.easier: Step feet out one at a time
modifications.harder: Faster pace, on forearms

exercises.name: Skater Hops
instructions: Stand on one leg with a slight bend.
Push off laterally, landing on the other foot in a soft skater pose.
Sweep the trailing leg behind for balance.
Repeat side to side.

modifications.harder: Wider lateral distance, faster pace

exercises.name: Stair Climb (Steady Pace)
instructions: Climb stairs at a steady, conversational pace.
Step on the full foot, drive through the heel.
Use the handrail lightly for balance, not for support.

modifications.harder: Increase pace, more flights

exercises.notes: Primary cardio option for users without outdoor access.

exercises.name: Stair Climb (Fast Pace)
instructions: Climb stairs as fast as you can while maintaining good form.
Use the handrail lightly for safety.
Walk down between sets for active recovery.
common_mistakes: Not using the handrail when fatigued

modifications.harder: Two-step climb, longer intervals

exercises.notes: HIIT-style stair work; very effective for cardio in limited time.

exercises.name: Two-Step Stair Climb
instructions: Climb stairs taking two steps at a time.
Drive through the heel of the lead foot.
Use the handrail for safety.
Walk down normally between sets.
common_mistakes: Pushing off the back foot instead of driving through the lead heel

modifications.easier: One step at a time at a fast pace
modifications.harder: Faster tempo

exercises.name: Stair Lunges
instructions: Stand 1-2 steps below a stair landing.
Lunge up onto a higher step, planting the lead foot fully.
Drive through the heel to bring the trailing foot up.
Step or walk back down between reps.

modifications.harder: Use a higher step or pause at the top

exercises.name: Stair Calf Raises
instructions: Stand on a stair edge with the balls of your feet on the step and heels hanging off.
Hold the handrail for balance.
Lower heels below the step for a deep stretch.
Press up onto the balls of the feet as high as possible.

modifications.harder: Single-leg version

exercises.notes: Use a stair with a sturdy handrail.

exercises.name: Stair Incline Push-Ups
instructions: Place hands on a stair edge, arms straight.
Walk feet back into a plank.
Lower chest to the stair edge.
Press back up.

modifications.harder: Lower stair, eventually floor

exercises.name: Stair Tricep Dips
instructions: Sit on a stair edge with hands gripping the edge next to your hips.
Slide off, supporting weight on your arms.
Bend elbows to lower hips toward the stair below.
Press back up.

modifications.harder: Legs extended straight

exercises.name: Stair Stride Descents
instructions: Walk down a flight of stairs with controlled, slightly slower-than-normal steps.
Land softly on the front of the foot.
Use the handrail for safety.
Walk back up between reps.

modifications.harder: Larger flights

exercises.notes: Eccentric quad work — very effective and underused.

exercises.name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
instructions: Stand tall.
Place one heel on a low surface (chair, step), leg straight.
Hinge from the hips and lean toward the extended foot.
Keep the back flat.
Switch sides.

modifications.easier: Both feet on the floor; reach for your toes
modifications.harder: Higher surface, deeper hinge

exercises.name: Standing Quad Stretch
instructions: Stand tall, one hand on a wall for balance.
Bend one knee and grab the foot behind you.
Pull the heel gently toward your glute.
Keep knees together, tuck pelvis under.
Switch sides.
common_mistakes: Knee pointing out to the side

modifications.easier: Lying on your side instead of standing
modifications.harder: Free-standing (no wall)

exercises.name: Pigeon Pose
instructions: Start on all fours.
Bring your right knee forward to just behind your right wrist; shin angled across the body.
Extend your left leg straight back, top of the foot on the floor.
Lower your hips toward the floor and fold forward over the front leg.
Switch sides.

modifications.easier: Figure-4 stretch on your back instead
modifications.harder: Deeper fold over the front leg

exercises.notes: Powerful release for tight hips from sitting.

exercises.name: Child's Pose
instructions: Kneel on the floor, big toes touching, knees apart.
Sit back on your heels.
Fold torso forward, extending arms in front of you.
Rest forehead on the floor.
Breathe deeply.
common_mistakes: Forcing the hips down if they don't reach the heels

modifications.easier: Place a pillow under hips or forehead
modifications.harder: Walk hands further forward for a deeper shoulder stretch

exercises.notes: Restorative pose; ideal for cooldowns.

exercises.name: Cat-Cow
instructions: Start on all fours, hands under shoulders, knees under hips.
Inhale: drop belly, lift chest and tailbone (cow).
Exhale: round the spine, tuck chin and tailbone (cat).
Move slowly with the breath.

modifications.harder: Add a hip circle on each side

exercises.notes: Foundational spinal mobility drill.

exercises.name: Cobra Pose
instructions: Lie face down, hands flat on the floor under shoulders.
Press through your hands to lift your chest off the floor.
Keep elbows slightly bent and shoulders away from ears.
Hips stay on the floor.
Look slightly forward.

modifications.harder: Higher lift, gaze upward

exercises.notes: Counter-pose to forward-flexed sitting posture.

exercises.name: Downward-Facing Dog
instructions: Start on all fours.
Tuck toes, lift hips up and back to form an inverted V.
Press the floor away with your hands; lengthen the spine.
Pedal the heels to feel a calf stretch.
Heels reach toward the floor (don't need to touch).

modifications.harder: Hold longer; lift one leg

exercises.name: Sun Salutation Flow (1 round)
instructions: Stand tall, hands at heart center.
Inhale: reach arms overhead.
Exhale: forward fold.
Inhale: half-lift (flat back).
Exhale: step or jump back to plank, lower halfway.
Inhale: upward dog or cobra.
Exhale: downward dog (hold 5 breaths).
Inhale: step forward to half-lift.
Exhale: forward fold.
Inhale: rise to standing, arms overhead.
Exhale: hands to heart center.

modifications.easier: Skip the chaturanga (lower halfway); use knees for the lower portion
modifications.harder: Add a lunge between forward fold and plank

exercises.notes: Excellent dynamic warmup or standalone short workout.

exercises.name: Thoracic Spine Rotation
instructions: Start on all fours.
Place your right hand behind your head, elbow out to the side.
Rotate your torso, bringing the right elbow toward the left arm.
Reverse: rotate up, opening the chest and pointing the right elbow to the ceiling.
Repeat, then switch sides.
common_mistakes: Twisting from the lower back instead of the upper back

modifications.harder: Hold the open position 3 seconds

exercises.notes: Critical for restoring thoracic mobility lost from desk work.

exercises.name: World's Greatest Stretch
instructions: Step into a deep lunge with the right foot forward.
Place left hand on the floor inside the right foot.
Rotate the right arm up toward the ceiling, opening the chest.
Return the right hand inside the foot.
Step back to start; switch sides.

modifications.easier: Hand on a chair or step instead of the floor
modifications.harder: Add a hamstring sweep (straighten the front leg between reps)

exercises.notes: Single best dynamic warmup for sedentary workers — covers everything.
